title: Post-CHG-0806 platform hygiene: re-baseline config, fix health-state staleness, trim MEMORY.md, run sandbox-boundary audit
description: Combined hygiene remediation triggered by standup Needs-Ken list. Addresses expected critical-config-baseline hash drift from CHG-0806 cron updates, stale health-state.json, MEMORY.md over hard limit, and overdue sandbox-boundary audit. Lower-priority items (untracked root .md files, cron timeout recommendations, L-102 env-wrapper, short :cloud cron timeouts, backup stale reconciliation) included if scope permits.
scope: Re-baseline state/critical-config-baseline.json after CHG-0806 cron payload changes
Investigate and fix state/health-state.json staleness (190min old; health_failure:1 in obs)
Trim MEMORY.md to ≤12K chars and archive overflow to memory/MEMORY-archive-2026-07-03.md
Run sandbox-boundary audit and report findings
Tidy untracked root .md files (DREAMS.md, yoda-daily-brief.md) per TKT-0341
Review and apply 5 auto-heal cron timeout recommendations (1 INCREASE, 4 DECREASE)
Reconcile backup-state stale alert (lastBackup vs lastSnap mismatch)
Evaluate L-102 gateway env-wrapper INERT status
risk: Low-to-medium. Health-state fix is operational-visibility critical. Config re-baseline is expected drift. MEMORY trim is safe if archive preserved.
rollback: Restore prior critical-config-baseline.json from backup-state snapshot. Revert MEMORY.md trim from archive file. Health-state fix can be reverted by restoring prior health-check cron/script versions.
